items: Quarter-end positioning followed Monday's technology-led rebound.
AI and semiconductor shares remained the dominant source of index-level momentum, while the market was still digesting June's valuation-driven AI selloff.
Chicago PMI and consumer-confidence data were scheduled; Nike earnings were due after the close.

intraday_and_postclose_catalysts: Semiconductors and AI infrastructure led another advance.
Oil eased while Treasury yields rose, producing a growth-positive but not fully broad risk-on tape.
Quarter-end buying helped trim June losses.
causal_narrative: The close of the quarter extended the AI rebound. Index strength was concentrated in semiconductors and AI infrastructure rather than a clean all-sector expansion. Easing oil helped risk appetite, while higher yields limited the breadth of the move.
source_notes: Swingfolio U.S. premarket and closing recap, 2026-06-30
alternative_explanations: Options and dealer positioning may amplify or oppose the public-news impulse.
Index concentration can make SPY and SPX diverge from equal-weight breadth.
